title: Okay Ive seen EoTV and EoE TOTALLY LOST
display_title: Okay Ive seen EoTV and EoE TOTALLY LOST
wikitext:
[]: Okay, I've seen EoTV and EoE, TOTALLY LOST             Quote Post by alih92 » Fri Jan 22, 2010 3:59 pm Okay I was fine with the tv series until the end, when the end came I felt like wow this show let me down a lot (like death note) but I watched some episodes that were my favorite (the intro and opening episodes are GREAT). As the series arrives at the conclusion point I found my self to be perfectly fine with the TV ending but because it wasn't a complete wrap up of the series it made me ANGRY (RAGE!!!) and I didn't want to watch the movie. Although I pushed myself to go and watch the movie. I watched it and this is what I've ascertained: -Rei betrays Gendo -Rei helps Shinji-kun -Rei dies, IDK HOW or WHY -Shinji decides to rebuild the world -Asuka is killed/or heavily wounded by the MP Evas, but HOW DOES SHE COME BACK? -At the last scene of EoE, is Asuka alive? -It seems that Shinji-kun (baka Shinji) has to repopulate the world with Asuka, but isn't she you know, DEAD?

[]: Re: Okay, I've seen EoTV and EoE, TOTALLY LOST             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Jan 22, 2010 4:06 pm -Rei dies, IDK HOW or WHY

[]:             Quote Post by alih92 » Fri Jan 22, 2010 4:10 pm can someone explain the difference between and the definitons of the complementation theory and the instrumentality theory?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Jan 22, 2010 4:15 pm alih92 wrote: can someone explain the difference between and the definitons of the complementation theory and the instrumentality theory?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Jan 22, 2010 4:16 pm alih92 wrote: can someone explain the difference between and the definitons of the complementation theory and the instrumentality theory?

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Fri Jan 22, 2010 4:18 pm If I'm not mistaken, Complementation and Instrumentality are essentially the same thing. Though it seems that GEHIRN/NERV itself really never called it "complementation", while it seems that SEELE used the terms synonomously. If I'm not mistaken, In episode 2 Gendo gets a folder of sorts labeled "Human Complementation Project", but I can't readily recall it used in dialogue.

[]: Re: Okay, I've seen EoTV and EoE, TOTALLY LOST             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jan 22, 2010 4:29 pm alih92 wrote: -At the last scene of EoE, is Asuka alive?

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Fri Jan 22, 2010 4:38 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Now whether she wanted to be dead or not, that's at least one of the recurrent arguments.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Jan 22, 2010 4:42 pm ran1 wrote: Mr. Tines wrote: Now whether she wanted to be dead or not, that's at least one of the recurrent arguments.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jan 22, 2010 4:42 pm @ran1 This thread, for example : http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=7776 @Xard in during ...

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Fri Jan 22, 2010 4:56 pm This thread, for example : http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=7776

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Jan 22, 2010 5:03 pm ^ Mr. Tines is our leading EoE hater (or at least hater of ending. I guess it also extends to whole film due to what happened to Asuka) so keep that in mind - majority's opinion (though I don't think there's any dominating pov regarding how screwed S&A are when it comes to survival in the end... it's left up to viewer after all) is not that and I disagree with him, for example

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jan 22, 2010 5:09 pm Xard wrote: how screwed S&ampA are when it comes to survival in the end...

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jan 23, 2010 3:57 pm Ah. Yeah, the filmmakers are using completely different techniques in telling their story. The TV series was written as a linear script (for the most part. The dream sequences on the train stray from that a bit). That means that one event leads into the other event in a very literal and chronological order. In End of Evangelion , the script does the same thing. Except this time, the script goes into Shinji's head for a bit, where everything is all disjointed, messed up, and confusing. Moments from the past are brought back up and remembered differently, or Shinji will fantasize about how past events could have gone differently. Then the script will return to the literal world where we see Rei rejecting Gendo and stuff. So basically, if you just separate Shinji's mind-trips from the literal events occurring in the film, they're easier to interpret. Then put the two back together, and see how what's happening to Shinji's mentality complements and even causes what's literally occurring. By the time Shinji finally exits the Eva and rises out of the water, he's done being mentally crazy, and the script finishes in the literal world.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jan 23, 2010 4:01 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: By the time Shinji finally exits the Eva and rises out of the water, he's done being mentally crazy

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jan 23, 2010 4:13 pm More strictly, shocked at the broken wreck that was all that remained of Asuka (and of the Earth).

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jan 23, 2010 4:42 pm He probably expected to end the same way the series did, with the cast successfully uploaded to a new realm of pure thought. nb4 Essel's 1-liner potshot

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jan 23, 2010 5:04 pm Assuming that's what actually happened.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jan 23, 2010 5:31 pm AuraTwilight wrote: I don't get what you mean by shocked. Were you expecting Shinji's rejection of Instrumentality to magically rewind time so the Earth is still green and pretty?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jan 23, 2010 7:32 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: By the time Shinji finally exits the Eva and rises out of the water, he's done being mentally crazy. Mr. Tines wrote: Are you 100% sure about that?

As the series arrives at the conclusion point I found my self to be perfectly fine with the TV en
